2605.30070.title: A Predictive Law for On-Policy Self-Distillation From World Feedback
2605.30070.paper_summary: Moving beyond simple scalar rewards toward richer world feedback is a natural path to more scalable RL post-training. On-policy self-distillation (OPSD) is a promising recent approach that uses arbitrary feedback as learning signal, yet its reliability compared to established methods, such as GRPO, remains unclear. We identify a strikingly consistent linear correlation between the initial student-self-teacher performance gap and the final performance improvement in OPSD. This relationship holds across context types and model families, providing a powerful predictive law for anticipating the outcome of an OPSD configuration without running the full training procedure. Interestingly, we show that this linear predictability holds with model scale, suggesting a potential basis for new empirical scaling laws on larger models with stronger in-context learning capabilities. In essence, our findings show that OPSD performance can be predicted and tuned before training, offering a principled way to incorporate world feedback as a first-class component of the post-training pipeline.
2605.30070.paper_summary_zh: 超越簡單的標量獎勵，朝向更豐富的世界反饋，是一條自然的道路，通向更具可擴展性的強化學習後訓練。政策內自我蒸餾（OPSD）是一種有前景的近期方法，利用任意反饋作為學習信號，但其相對於已建立方法（如 GRPO）的可靠性仍不明朗。我們發現初始學生自我教師表現差距與 OPSD 最終表現提升之間存在驚人且一致的線性相關性。這種關係在不同的上下文類型和模型家族中均成立，提供了一種強大的預測法則，用於預測 OPSD 配置的結果，而無需運行完整的訓練過程。有趣的是，我們顯示這種線性可預測性隨著模型規模的增長而保持，這暗示著在具有更強上下文學習能力的更大模型上，可能存在新的經驗性擴展法則的基礎。總之，我們的研究結果顯示，OPSD 的表現可以在訓練之前進行預測和調整，提供了一種原則性的方法，將世界反饋作為後訓練流程的一級組件納入。
2605.30070.author: Tommy He et.al.
2605.30070.authors: Tommy He, Jerome Sieber, Matteo Saponati
